title: Extended: incidentSummary (v0.1)
description: Audience-neutral summary of incidents that occurred within the Pack's scope. The taxonomy of severities, categories, and statuses is OPEN — implementers use whatever framework their incident-management practice prescribes (ITIL, NIST IR, AI Act categories, internal taxonomies). Carried under the Pack envelope's 'extended.incidentSummary' field.

period.description: Period this summary covers. SHOULD equal or be a subset of extended.scope.timeWindow.

total.description: Total number of incidents in the period. Zero is a meaningful claim — 'zero incidents in scope' is itself an attestation, not an absence of data.

bySeverity.description: Counts keyed by severity label. Open keys — no locked taxonomy. Conventional labels: 'critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low', 'informational'.

byCategory.description: Counts keyed by category. Open keys. Conventional: 'policy_violation', 'safety', 'data_quality', 'operational_failure', 'security'.

byStatus.description: Counts keyed by resolution status. Open keys. Conventional: 'open', 'investigating', 'resolved', 'closed_no_action'.

incidents.description: Individual incident records. Open shape per item. Conventional fields: 'id', 'category', 'severity', 'occurredAt', 'status', 'summary', 'eventRefs' (eventIds in this Pack that document the triggering events). MAY be a subset of the total — e.g. critical/high incidents listed individually, lower severities aggregated only in counters.
